# SkySlope Forms — comprehensive file log

Generated (UTC): 2026-04-11T00:02:58.147Z

**Scope:** SkySlope **Forms** transaction files via `https://api-latest.skyslope.com` (`GET /api/files/listings` and `GET /api/files/sales`). This is the **Forms file cabinet** integration surface, **not** SkySlope Suite (different product). Archived folder rows excluded unless `SKYSLOPE_INCLUDE_ARCHIVED=1`.
**Inventory accuracy:** Folder lists use swagger-correct query params (`earliestDate` / `latestDate` as Unix seconds, `pageNumber`, **10 rows per page**). Older scripts that used `fromDate` / `page` / `pageSize` stopped after the first page and under-counted folders.
**You are the final say** on whether a document is fully executed and on any rename. This log is an **educated first pass** from PDF text plus filenames.

## Standardized naming (for the upcoming rename pass)

Pattern: `YYYY-MM-DD__LIST|sale__MLS-{mls|none}__{category}__{seq}__{sanitized-original-stem}.pdf` where **seq** is chronological order within that folder (001, 002, …). **category** uses the inferred type below (e.g. `buyer-offer-or-package`, `numbered-counter`, `listing-agreement`). After you review, you can shorten categories or add a human token like `WAVE-02` between MLS and category for offer rounds.

| 13 | 2025-10-04 | earnest_or_wire | `2025-10-04__LIST__MLS-220197955__earnest-or-wire__013__Offer1-Wire_Fraud_Advisory_-_218_ts03966.pdf` | 1 | **see_pdf** — Classify obligations by form type; verify in viewer. | Glen Reese Susan Reese | ok | WIRE FRAUD ADVISORY Wire fraud has become a wide spread issue impacting real estate transactions. Buyers and sellers need to exercise extreme caution when wiring funds in real estate transactions. Criminals/hackers target email accounts of parties to a real estate transaction, including but not limited to, real estate brokers, title/escrow officers and mortgage brokers. In many cases they have been able to intercept emailed wire transfer instructions, obtain account information and, by altering some of the data, use emails to redirect the funds to a different account. These scams are extremely sophisticated and appear as if they are coming from one of the parties in your real estate transact… |

Buyers and sellers need to exercise extreme caution when wiring funds in real estate transactions. Criminals/hackers target email accounts of parties to a real estate transaction, including but not limited to, real estate brokers, title/escrow officers and mortgage brokers. In many cases they have been able to intercept emailed wire transfer instructions, obtain account information and, by altering some of the data, use emails to redirect the funds to a different account. These scams are extremely sophisticated and appear as if they are coming from one of the parties in your real estate transacti… |

Buyers and sellers need to exercise extreme caution when wiring funds in real estate transactions. Criminals/hackers target email accounts of parties to a real estate transaction, including but not limited to, real estate brokers, title/escrow officers and mortgage brokers. In many cases they have been able to intercept emailed wire transfer instructions, obtain account information and, by altering some of the data, use emails to redirect the funds to a different account. Buyers and sellers need to exercise extreme caution when wiring funds in real estate transactions. Criminals/hackers target email accounts of parties to a real estate transaction, including but not limited to, real estate brokers, title/escrow officers and mortgage brokers. In many cases they have been able to intercept emailed wire transfer instructions, obtain account information and, by altering some of the data, use emails to redirect the funds to a different account. These scams are extremely sophisticated and appear as if they are coming from one of the parties in your real estate transacti… |

| 35 | 2025-09-06 | agency_disclosure_pamphlet | `2025-09-06__SALE__MLS-220200647__agency-disclosure-pamphlet__035__Initial_Agency_Pamphlet.pdf` | 3 | **likely_seller_executed** — E-sign or signature labels present; confirm seller/firm lines visually. | — | ok | OREGON REAL ESTATE AGENCY Initial Agency Disclosure Pamphlet Consumers: This pamphlet describes the legal obligations of Oregon real estate licensees to consum- ers. Real estate brokers and principal real estate brokers are required to provide this information to you when they first contact you. A licensed real estate broker or principal broker need not provide the pamphlet to a party who has, or may be reasonably assumed to have, received a copy of the pam- phlet from another broker. | 36 | 2025-09-06 | earnest_or_wire | `2025-09-06__SALE__MLS-220200647__earnest-or-wire__036__Wire_Fraud_Advisory.pdf` | 2 | **see_pdf** — Classify obligations by form type; verify in viewer. | Elsa Uchikawa | ok | WIRE FRAUD ADVISORY Wire fraud has become a widespread issue impacting real estate transactions. Buyers and sellers need to exercise extreme caution when wiring funds in real estate transactions. Criminals/hackers target email accounts of parties to a real estate transaction, including but not limited to, real estate brokers, title/escrow officers and mortgage brokers. In many cases they have been able to intercept emailed wire transfer instructions, obtain account information and, by altering some of the data, use emails to redirect the funds to a different account. These scams are extremely sophisticated and appear as if they are coming from one of the parties in your real estate transacti… |

Nor is the upstairs ADU permitted for use as an ADU. These findings were later confirmed by our agent during a visit to the city office.
